@@ -227,2 +227,19 @@
   - Held A/B/C prices at $89/$229/$399 and model tiers at A3/B3/C4.
 - Watch next week: cash after the $500k R&D hit, whether S2 conversion holds under higher ad spend, compute under tier 3 A, issue age, and R&D tier 2 expected completion around day 93.
+
+## Week 15 / Day 98
+
+- Cash: $1,528,921. Subscribers: 51,088. Week 14 added 14,475 subscribers from 26,916 leads; cash increased by $382,773 even after the prior $500k R&D spend.
+- Tier 2 R&D completed, raising global bonus to 0.2288. S2 A quality is now 1.1196.
+- S2 content remains strong: 23,582 content A leads and 12,735 content A subscribers, about 54.0% conversion at A $89.
+- Week 14 cash flow: subscription payments $1,732,396; compute -$297,738; ops -$276,500; ads -$175,000; capacity -$70,000; dev -$16,800; lead acquisition -$13,585.
+- Issues: S2 has 4,549 open issues, avg age 0.31 days, max 41. Volume is high, but the average age remains fresh. S1 has a few very old issues but little commercial value.
+- Changes:
+  - Started R&D tier 4 for $666,667. Sampled expected completion day 128, expected boost +0.1078.
+  - Re-tested A/B/C prices at $99/$249/$449 now that A is tier 3 and R&D tier 2 has landed.
+  - Raised quotas to A 25000, B 100000, C 250000.
+  - Scaled S2 content ads to $40,000/day.
+  - Increased ops to $55,500/day total: global $12,000, S1 $1,000, S2 $42,000, S3 $500.
+  - Increased S2 targeted dev slightly; total dev is $2,900/day.
+  - Held capacity at tier 5; current usage is well below the 25M/day limit.
+- Watch next week: whether A $99 now holds conversion with higher quality, cash after tier 4 R&D, issue age under heavier volume, compute, and tier 5 headroom.
